item.title: DepEd orders simple, meaningful graduation rites amid rising fuel prices
item.description: The Department of Education (DepEd) on Monday has directed all public schools nationwide to hold simple, meaningful, and frugal graduation and moving-up ceremonies for School Year 2025–2026, as families grapple with rising fuel and commodity prices.

item.title: Tiangco-Castro clash highlights tensions over Duterte impeachment push
item.description: Navotas Lone District Rep. Toby Tiangco on Monday expressed surprise over Presidential Communications Office Undersecretary Claire Castro’s criticism of his claims involving fellow lawmakers and the push to impeach Vice President Sara Duterte.

item.title: Leviste denies admitting to causing Castro ‘sleepless nights and anxiety’
item.description: Batangas 1st District Rep. Leandro Legarda Leviste has denied admitting to having caused Presidential Communications Office Undersecretary (PCO) Claire Castro “sleepless nights and anxiety,” explaining he only cited Castro in her affidavit.

item.title: China ‘clearing ops’ at Bajo de Masinloc meant to intimidate Filipino fishermen – PCG
item.description: The China Coast Guard (CCG) declared so-called “clearing operations” in parts of the vicinity of Bajo de Masinloc or Scarborough Shoal on Monday, warning vessels within a specified area to leave between 11 a.m. and 6 p.m.

item.title: Bus operators reduce trips as fuel costs continue to rise
item.description: A bus company in Cubao, Quezon City is waiting for enough passengers before trips in an effort to cut diesel costs, resulting in nearly half of its trips per route being reduced.

item.title: Rhian Ramos to pursue possible legal action over driver’s assault claims
item.description: Actress Rhian Ramos said she is already looking into possible legal actions against her former driver, who alleged that she, Michelle Dee, and Sam Panlilio physically assaulted and illegally detained him back in January.

item.title: ‘I am deeply sorry’: Suntay’s wife breaks silence on husband's Anne Curtis remark
item.description: The wife of Rep. Bong Suntay has issued a public statement distancing herself and her children from the controversial remarks the Quezon City lawmaker made during a House impeachment hearing, after his comments referencing actress Anne Curtis sparked widespread backlash online.

item.title: No double standard in impeachment proceedings — House justice panel members
item.description: Leaders of the House Committee on Justice asserted there was no double standard in its deliberations on the impeachment complaints against the country’s two highest officials.

item.title: DSWD urges stronger cooperation among ASEAN states amid global tensions
item.description: Social Welfare Secretary Rex Gatchalian called on other members of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) for stronger cooperation amid global headwinds.

item.title: Man wanted for inciting to sedition surrenders to police
item.description: The Quezon City Police District on Thursday said it has successfully served a warrant of arrest against an individual wanted for inciting to sedition.
